title: [LowerSeqToSV] Use a dynamic index for array registers if possible
body:
This PR tries lowering firrtl subaccess operation into SV variable
index assignment when possible. Because of the last connection sematnics,
it is not always possible to reconstruct subaccess operations. Specifically we can 
reconstruct subaccess only if there is no other write in the control flow path. Since bin flags are necessary for this transformation correctness, https://github.com/llvm/circt/pull/4689/commits/72c559ed67642ef5a466ea8fce5ace3c52bf2f07 propagates more bin flags thorough and op and icmp op canonicalizers.
```scala
circuit Example:
  module Example:
    input clock: Clock
    input en: UInt<1>
    input addr: UInt<2>
    input data: UInt<32>
    output out: UInt<32>[3]
    reg r : UInt<32>[3], clock

    when en:
      r[addr] <= data
    out <= r
```

```verilog
; $firtool -preserve-aggregate=1d-vec -preserve-public-types=false -disable-reg-randomization
module Mem(
  input              clock,
                     en,
  input  [1:0]       addr,
  input  [31:0]      data,
  output [2:0][31:0] out
);

  reg [2:0][31:0] r;
  always @(posedge clock) begin
    if (en)
      r[addr] <= data;
  end // always @(posedge)
  assign out = r;
endmodule
```
